## Summary

Adds the next top-down deficiency item: a plain-language Security & Access Controls page.

## Why

The deficiency roadmap calls for a security/privacy trust surface covering account access, manuscript storage, export/download controls, logged Storygate activity, and no public indexing.

## Changes

- Adds `/security` as a dedicated public trust page.
- Explains the author-facing security posture:
  - private by default
  - shared by author action
  - controlled Storygate access
- Adds security commitments for:
  - account-gated workspaces
  - manuscript storage boundaries
  - controlled downloads
  - logged controlled access
- Defines access boundaries:
  - private workspace
  - export boundary
  - Storygate boundary
  - admin boundary
- Adds author controls around evaluation, report review, Revise decisions, downloads, and package preparation.
- Adds precision guardrails so the page does not claim unsupported certifications or current film/TV/adaptation routing.
- Adds a plain-language Security FAQ.

## Scope

Public trust copy only.

No auth, database, storage, permissions, security implementation, Storygate runtime, Agent Readiness runtime, evaluation, report, or pipeline behavior changed.

## Note

This is not a formal security policy or compliance certification page. It is a user-facing trust page. Formal legal/security policy pages can still be added separately.
